Expenditures charged to the Fund shall always give priority to the projects enumerated in Section 272 of Republic Act 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B. Further, expenditures of schools from far flung barangays receiving smaller budget from DepEd should be given priority other than answering the expenditures of the biggest schools of the Municipality receiving bigger budgets from DepEd.
observations.reason: Not Reiterated in Finding No. 9 | Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements to the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days from the receipt of the Bank Statements as required under Sections 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-001 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 74 of PD 1445.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 7 | Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the HRMO to furnish the Audit Team with copies of contracts for JOs within five days after its approval in order to facilitate review thereof. Ensure that conditions are clearly stated in the contracts of Service and specify the following: a) expected outputs to facilitate comparison with the accomplishment report; b) specific period within which the work is to be accomplished; and c) source of funds for the payment of salaries.

We recommended that Management create an Inspection/Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the PPE owned by the municipality and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1 in order for us to determine the validity and existence of the PPEs owned by the Municipality. Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain, reconcile and update the records of the PPE.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the limitations set forth in SEF pursuant to Section 99, 100 and 272 of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM, DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01-01-A and 01-B, and refrain from charging expenditures which are not related to the operation and maintenance of elementary and secondary public schools against the Special Education Fund.

1.6		We recommend the management to create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. to determine the existence and accuracy of the PPE owned by the LGU. Moreover, direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards and issue Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment, and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger Cards as required in Section 114 of the same Manual.

2.5	We recommend the management to adhere strictly the provision of Section 379 of RA No. 7160 and Section 2 of PD No. 1445 on the filing of an application of the Property Officer/Municipal Treasurer for inspection and disposal of the unserviceable government property to COA Auditor and state policy on all resources of the government, respectively.  Further, create an Inspection and Disposal Committee to take charge the condition of the owned property of the LGU.

3.4 	We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer shall record the financial transactions of the Municipality daily pursuant to Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II. Likewise, direct her to update and balance all the transactions and reconcile the same with the cash on hand as provided in Section 63 (D) of LTOM. Moreover, direct the Liquidating Officer to maintain Liquidating Officer’s Cashbook for each fund which shall be updated and balanced daily as provided for in Section 63 (B) of LTOM and all the Revenue Collectors shall maintain Teller’s/ Collector’s to record all collections received and all remittances to the designated Liquidating Officer in accordance with Section 63 (C) of LTOM.

4.7		We recommend the management to compel the Municipal Accountant to see to it that reconciliation is made between the balance shown in the reports of the depositories and the balance found in the books of the agency pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445. Moreover, require her to prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements within ten (10) days from receipt of the bank statements and submit the same to Auditor concerned as provided for in Section 3.2 of COA Circular No.   96-011. Also, direct her to furnish the Auditor copies of Accountant’s Advice of Local Check Disbursements to ensure that checks encashed by government depository banks are for legitimate local government expenditures.

5.10	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to ensure the proper granting and utilization of all cash advances as provided in Section 8 of COA Circular No. 97-002. Also, require the accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate and/ or settle their overdue outstanding cash advances. Otherwise, such failure / or settle the same shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of their salaries and the imposition of other sanctions. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly update the Monthly and Quarterly Monitoring Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances Granted to Accountable Officers including cash advances for salaries, wages and other specific purpose supported with liquidation report promptly for immediate review and submit the same on or before 5th day of the following month. Besides, require her to attach a certification that the previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before.

6.7	We recommend the Municipal Local School Board to prepare                      Annual School Board Budget for the SEF and adhere strictly the prioritize programs, projects and activities (PPAs) pursuant to R.A No. 5447, Section 272 of RA 7160 and Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG.  Stop using the SEF to PPAs not within the list of priority expenses as provided in the latter’s provisions. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to stop disbursing funds without approved appropriation pursuant to Section 4 (1) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

7.4	In view of the above, we recommend the Local School Board to maximize the utilization of the SEF based on RA 5447 as well as Section 272 of RA 7160 and LOI 1462 for the benefits of its intended beneficiaries. Further, we recommend that the Local School Board initiate the inventory of local school buildings that need urgent construction/repairs, operation of such number of extension classes as may be needed to accommodate all children of school and other school needs like books, science and mathematics aids, and simple laboratory devices to help identify the priority PPAs.

8.5	We recommend the management to strictly adhere RA 9003 to adopt a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management programs. Further, prepare the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan consistent with the national solid waste management for approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to have efficient and effective implementation.
8.6	Likewise, please submit the Approved 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and Status of Implementation of the approved 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan.

11.10	We recommend the management should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Section 2 of RA 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002 in the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF. Stop practicing of disbursement charged to LDRRMF expenses which are not related to and do not support disaster risk management activities. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to stop payment not related to LDRRMF programs and activities.

12.5		We recommend the management to implement and utilize the LCPC Fund to its programs, projects and activities (PPAs). Direct the members of the LCPC to prepare Local Development Plan for Children and Local Investment Plan for Children, and maximize the allocated LCPC Fund to identified PPAs provided for in DILG MC No 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 to ensure full strengthening.

13.8	We recommend that Management should strictly adhere in the provision under Presidential Decree No.  1445. Further, we recommend that management conduct competitive public bidding for all procurement which is not covered by Rule XVI of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act 9184 pursuant to section 10 of the same IRR. And adhere strictly to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

14.4	We recommend management to strictly adhere Section 7, Rule II of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and prepare PPMP and consolidate APP. Require the Bids and Award Committee to furnish the Office of the Auditor of the updated PPMP and APP yearly for validation and assurance that all procurement undertaken by the LGU were based on the approved annual procurement plan.

15.6	We recommend the management to require the GAD Focal Person to prepare the Annual GPB using the prescribed form and in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, submit it to the Provincial Planning and Development Office then to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval. Further, develop GAD PAPs to strengthen the Municipality’s gender-responsive governance and ensure that GAD Fund is utilized in align with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended and Management agreed to immediately designate an employee who shall be responsible person to facilitate the registration and insurance of all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality. Apply and/or renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the municipality with LTO and contracts of insurance with the GSIS to avoid additional expenses in terms of penalties and payment of damages to third party liabilities in case of vehicular accidents.

We recommended and Management agreed to exert effort to continuously implement the program on schedule so that the impact of the program will be measured and the objectives of the program will be attained. Further, the Management instructed the MSWDO for early and immediate submission of the necessary reports for the liquidation of the fund in order to avail of new funding.

We recommended and Management agreed to require MOA before any financial assistance will be released to qualified recipients. The MOA shall cover the purposes and all the conditions in the implementation of the proposed programs/projects/ activities by the recipients. This will be the basis in granting financial assistance and in monitoring of the utilization of the fund to make certain that funds will be properly used for its intended purpose. Further, it is recommended that the fund transfer shall be first taken up in the books of accounts as a receivable and be recorded to Other MOOE or Donations after receipt and evaluation of liquidation.

We further recommended and Management agreed to require the recipients of the financial assistance to liquidate and account the amount transferred to their agencies and associations supported with complete pertinent documents.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement minimum and standard requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collections and their final disposal to the dumpsite, as follows: a) Separate containers /  receptacles  for all types of wastes shall be maintained from all sources; b) These containers must be properly marked or identified for on-site collections such as  compostable, non- recyclable, recyclable, special waste and/or any other classification as may be determined by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; c) Maintain receptacles and Materials Recovery Facility for temporary placement of solid wastes; and d) Convert the open dump to a controlled dump site.

We recommended and Management agreed to prepare budgets based on the duly approved Annual Investment Plan indicating each program, project and activities and identify the expected outputs for each PPAs. This shall be presented in detail stating therein the details of the projects including the exact location. For appropriations already presented in lump sum or in generic terms, a separate SB appropriation ordinance/resolution should always be secured by the Municipal Mayor to serve as an authority to enter into contract in behalf of the Municipal Government in accordance to Section 22 (c) of R.A. 7160 and pertinent rulings of G.R. No. 175527 dated December 8, 2008.
We recommended and Management agreed to fully utilize the 20% development Fund for the attainment of its objectives to the advantage of the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere with the provisions of COA, DBM, DILG, Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015. Further, we recommended and Management agreed to stop the granting of additional cash advance for Confidential Fund to the Municipal Mayor or Special Disbursing Officer unless the liquidation of previous cash advances for CY 2015 have been submitted to the COA-ICFAU for audit. The Municipal Accountant shall stamp or print on the DV that the previous cash advances for the same purpose, project or activity given to the Municipal Mayor requesting cash advance have been liquidated and proper accounting thereof was made.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all barangay shares from the Real Property and Community Tax Certificate collections are remitted to the LGUs concerned within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160.
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stop releasing and paying wages of personnel under the “job order” status without the required Accomplishment Report.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of allowing job order personnel to travel outside the Municipality with travel allowances and/or per diems. Authority to travel should be granted only when these are urgent, extremely necessary, involves the minimum expenditure and are beneficial to the agency concerned and/or the country without resulting to depletion of LGU resources, increased MOOE and wastage of government funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of paying salaries to employees without Daily Time Records and Approved Applications for Leave, require each and every official and employee to attach the individual daily time record to the payroll/voucher, with approved applications for leave before payment of salaries and wages shall be made, and ensure that all absences without pay must be properly deducted from the payroll.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to conduct pre-procurement and pre-bid conferences or as the need arises to clarify terms and conditions and specifications stipulated in the bidding documents to enhance transparency and in compliance with the implementing rules and regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain subsidiary Ledgers on the different funds received by the LGU to strengthen the accounting controls over the transactions on all funds of the LGU and necessary information on a particular fund from a certain source agency can be gathered and validated by the end users as well as the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the contractor to refund the amount of P342,919.20 representing the overpayment made to him and/or deduct said amount from any existing claims of the contractor. The Municipal Engineer should validate through actual inspection the accomplishment report submitted by the contractor to ensure that actual accomplishment is in accordance with the program of works, plans and specifications to avoid overpayment to contractors. This may generate savings which can be utilized for the implementation of other priority projects related to the construction of new municipal building.

We recommended and Management agreed to support with the required complete documents the disbursement vouchers pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Otherwise, a Notice of Suspension will be issued.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Health Officer to attach a certification that the drugs and medicines procured fall within and conform to PNDF Volume I, current edition in compliance with Paragraph II.2 of DOH AO No. 163 s. 2002.

We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from entering into contracts which are not represented by Municipal Mayor being the head of the agency. Adhere strictly to the provisions of Sections 2 and 4(5) of PD 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere strictly to the provisions of Philippine Commission on Women, Department of Interior and Local Government, Department of Budget and Management and National Economic and Development Authority Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. GAD Focal Point System shall be established tasked to promote gender-responsive governance, protect and fulfill women’s human rights, and promote women’s economic development.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the GAD focal person of the Municipality be directed to submit the GAD Plan, Accomplishment Report and reports to DILG Regional Office and furnish the same the Office of the Auditor pursuant to DILG-LBM No. 28 dated June 15, 1997 and Items 4.1.A.4).g and 4.1.C.5.1) and 2) of Philippine Commission on Women, Department of Interior and Local Government, Department of Budget and Management and National Economic and Development Authority Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01

We recommended that Management conduct physical count of the PPEs owned by the municipality, issue Acknowledgement Report for Equipment of all items/units to determine and establish the accountability, responsibility and liability of the accountable officers who have custody of the government properties. Further, for new requisitions, ARE should be prepared prior to issuance of the items to the end user.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of charging the expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures to the 20% development fund. The Municipal officials shall strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of paying salaries to employees without Daily Time Records and Approved Applications for Leave, require each and every official and employee to attach the individual daily time record to the payroll/voucher, with approved applications for leave before payment of salaries and wages shall be made, and ensure that all absences without pay must be properly deducted from the payroll.

We recommended that Management prepare the Driver’s Trip Tickets, Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption in the use of government vehicles as required in Section 361 of GAAM Volume 1.

We recommended that Management conduct pre-repair inspection prior to any repair and requisition of spare parts. The inspection conducted should be properly and completely documented. All deliveries of items purchased must first be received by the General Services Officer.

We recommended and Management agreed stop the practice of splitting requisitions by dividing it into small segments. To remedy the adverse condition, Management must have to adhere strictly on the general rules for the procurement of supplies and materials pursuant to COA Circular No. 76-41 and RA 9184 to ensure efficiency and economy in the LGUs procurement process.

We recommended that the GAD focal person of the Municipality be directed to submit the GAD Plan and reports to the NCRFW and to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to DBM,NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1, COA Memorandum No. 2009 - 020 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
